report_name: Vulnerabilities Surge 51% as AI-Driven Supply Chain Attacks Rise

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Vercel Attackers
threat_actors.description: The threat actors responsible for the Vercel OAuth breach. They utilized Lumma Stealer to compromise a Context.ai employee's machine, steal credentials, and then abuse a trusted third-party application relationship to access Vercel's systems.
threat_actors.goals: Credential theft and unauthorized access to cloud platforms.

threat_actors.name: Generic Threat Actors
threat_actors.description: General threat actors mentioned in the Forescout report who are increasingly operationalizing AI to accelerate attack campaigns and exploiting legacy vulnerabilities.
threat_actors.goals: Exploitation of vulnerabilities for various malicious purposes, including ransomware deployment.

threat_actors.name: State-affiliated actors from China
threat_actors.description: Threat actors linked to China, accounting for a portion of 32% of notable malicious activity observed in H1 2026.

threat_actors.name: State-affiliated actors from Russia
threat_actors.description: Threat actors linked to Russia, accounting for a portion of 32% of notable malicious activity observed in H1 2026.

threat_actors.name: State-affiliated actors from Iran
threat_actors.description: Threat actors linked to Iran, accounting for a portion of 32% of notable malicious activity observed in H1 2026.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: Lumma Stealer was used to compromise a Context.ai employee's machine and steal credentials, including Google Workspace OAuth tokens.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: Attackers abused the trusted relationship between Vercel and the integrated third-party application from Context.ai, using a stolen OAuth token to gain access to Vercel's environment.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used a stolen OAuth token to impersonate a legitimate employee, masking their malicious activity as legitimate API traffic from a trusted service.

attack_patterns.name: Generative AI
attack_patterns.description: The report highlights a trend of threat actors operationalizing AI to accelerate attack campaigns, such as reducing the time from vulnerability disclosure to exploitation.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: PowerShell is often used by threat actors to execute info-stealing payloads, frequently using encoded commands to obfuscate their actions.

attack_patterns.name: Rundll32
attack_patterns.description: Rundll32.exe can be used by attackers to proxy the execution of malicious code. Its execution by non-standard parent processes, such as a web browser, is a potential indicator of compromise.

malware.name: Lumma Stealer
malware.description: Info-stealer malware used in the Vercel breach to compromise a Context.ai employee's machine and steal credentials, including Google Workspace OAuth tokens.

identities.name: Vercel
identities.description: A major developer platform that was breached by attackers who gained access by abusing a trusted OAuth integration with Context.ai.

identities.name: Context.ai
identities.description: A third-party application provider whose employee was compromised by Lumma Stealer, leading to the theft of an OAuth token that was subsequently used to access Vercel's platform.

identities.name: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)
identities.description: Mentioned in the report for adding 146 vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog during the first half of 2026.

indicators.name: Anomalous OAuth Token Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous OAuth Token Activity
id: 2f8b1c9a-7e4d-4f6a-8b3c-1d9a7b0c6d5e
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects unusual OAuth token activity, such as tokens generated or used from unexpected geographic locations, at unusual times, or with a high frequency of requests. This could indicate a compromised account or abuse of a trusted application integration, as seen in the Vercel breach.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/industrial-cyber-attacks/forescout-reports-51-surge-in-vulnerabilities-as-ai-supply-chain-attacks-drive-threats-across-iot-ot-infrastructure/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-22
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.t1199
level: medium
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.azuread
detection:
  selection:
    OperationName: 'Add service principal credentials'
    Result: 'success'
  filter_known_locations:
    IpAddress|cidr:
      - 192.0.2.0/24 # Replace with known corporate IP ranges
  condition: selection and not filter_known_locations
indicators.description: Detects unusual OAuth token activity in cloud audit logs, such as tokens generated outside of expected geographic locations or business hours, which could indicate abuse of trusted relationships.

indicators.name: Suspicious PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
id: 8c3d1a9b-5e6f-4a7b-9c8d-2e1f0a9b8c7d
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects the execution of PowerShell with an encoded command argument. This is a common technique used by malware like info-stealers to obfuscate and execute malicious payloads.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/industrial-cyber-attacks/forescout-reports-51-surge-in-vulnerabilities-as-ai-supply-chain-attacks-drive-threats-across-iot-ot-infrastructure/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-22
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -encodedcommand '
      - ' -e '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PowerShell with encoded commands, a technique often used by info-stealers to hide malicious payloads.

indicators.name: Anomalous User-Agent for Credential Theft
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous User-Agent for Credential Theft
id: 4a9b8c7d-3e2f-4d6a-8b9c-1d0e9f8a7b6c
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects web requests with an empty or non-standard user agent. Info-stealers like Lumma Stealer may exhibit this behavior when exfiltrating data.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/industrial-cyber-attacks/forescout-reports-51-surge-in-vulnerabilities-as-ai-supply-chain-attacks-drive-threats-across-iot-ot-infrastructure/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-22
tags:
  - attack.t1555.003
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-useragent: ''
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects web requests with an empty or non-standard user agent, which can be an indicator of info-stealer activity like Lumma Stealer.

indicators.name: Suspicious Rundll32 Execution by Browser
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Rundll32 Execution by Browser
id: 1d9a7b0c-6d5e-4f8b-8c3d-9a7b0c6d5e4f
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects rundll32.exe being launched by a web browser, which is anomalous behavior and may indicate an attempt to execute malicious code downloaded from the internet.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/industrial-cyber-attacks/forescout-reports-51-surge-in-vulnerabilities-as-ai-supply-chain-attacks-drive-threats-across-iot-ot-infrastructure/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-22
tags:
  - attack.t1218.011
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\iexplore.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects rundll32.exe being spawned by a web browser, which can be an indicator of malicious code execution.
